reviews.review_title: Will be a great co-op game
reviews.review_description: Since this is an early access game, I was only able to play alone. I watched my friends playing beforehand and when it became my turn, I realized that the game was scarier than how it looked on screen. The screen in the main room says that the multiplayer mode will be updated soon and I'll be playing this game again with friends who have fought better than I did.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I love hearing that you experienced it both on screen and inside the headset
it really is scarier once it’s your turn.

And good news: 2-player co-op is already available. The multiplayer update has been rolled out, so you can jump back in with your friends and survive together.

It’s always different when you’re the one inside the chaos instead of watching from the outside.

Thanks for giving it a try, and I hope your next run with friends is even more intense.

reviews.review_description: So far the atmosphere is extremely scary.  it is a great single player game and even a fun party game if the casting mode is being used. I had  hard time passing through the tutorial. It would be better if the direction stays on the top/middle of the screen as it skips so fast. But the stages are fun and challenging.

developer_response.body: "Great to hear you're enjoying the scary atmosphere! It’s awesome that you’re using casting to turn it into a fun co-op style party experience. We’ve noted your feedback on the tutorial text speed and will work on it to help players get into the action faster. Good luck with your survival in the next stages of this zombie shooter!"

reviews.review_title: PLAY ALONE!!! with no one around u!
reviews.review_description:
Lowkey? This game slaps.
Hongdae looks good with those neon lights, clubs, all of it. Then boom, zombies randomly run at you. Gunplay is clean, movement feels pretty good, and the zombies are actually terrifying in the beggining. Story is whatever, but the street vibes are top tier. If you like fast shooters + K-zombie energy, it’s a yes.

**4/5 stars. Would get jump-scared again.** Looking forward their update.

developer_response.body: "Glad the Hongdae vibes hit the spot! 🌃 We wanted to capture that authentic K-zombie energy mixed with fast-paced action. It’s awesome to hear the shooting feels clean and the apocalypse atmosphere is terrifying. Whether you're playing for the story or just the survival thrill, we appreciate the support for our shooter. Watch out for those jumpscares!"

reviews.review_description:
So, I don’t usually write app reviews, but Zombirus impressed me enough to actually leave one. The game feels noticeably more realistic than other zombie titles I’ve tried—everything from the atmosphere to the way enemies move pulls you in.

The weapons are especially well done. The guns feel solid, the reload motions are smooth, and even the melee attacks have a nice weight to them. It’s clear the developers put real care into the combat system.

Overall, Zombirus delivers a surprisingly immersive experience and stands out among VR zombie games. Definitely worth checking out.

developer_response.body: "We truly appreciate you taking the time to write such a detailed review. It’s fantastic to hear that the realistic shooting mechanics and weapon weight stood out to you. Our goal was to create a zombie game that differentiates itself through immersive action and atmosphere. We are committed to making this the best VR survival shooter possible. Stay tuned for more updates!"

reviews.review_title: Review for this well-crafted Zombie game
reviews.review_description:
I enjoyed my first time with Zomvirus.

First, I really liked the Stage 1 environment and the zombie designs—they felt well-crafted and helped set the mood. The sound design also matched the game very well and added a lot to the overall atmosphere.

Second, I think players who are already familiar with firearms will find the gun handling intuitive. In particular, inserting the magazine and chambering a round before firing felt natural and straightforward.

Lastly, I have one constructive suggestion regarding the tutorial: I’d appreciate more on-screen subtitles or clearer guidance. For example, there was a prompt that said “go to the table,” but there were multiple tables, so it wasn’t clear which one to approach. It might help to specify the exact table or add a visual indicator (such as an arrow). Personally, I also struggled at first with the part where you need to holster the gun at your waist, so an additional explanation or a clearer cue there would be very helpful.

Overall, I’m excited to see how the game develops, and I’m looking forward to future updates.

developer_response.body: "Thank you for the constructive feedback and kind words about the zombie designs! We are actively working on improving the tutorial and UI guidance to make the shooting and survival mechanics more intuitive for everyone. Your input helps us refine the apocalypse experience. We hope you stick around to see how the action evolves in future updates!"

reviews.review_description: I've already died like four times just on the second stage, and I honestly don't know what I am doing wrong. Every time I go into the convenience store, zombies swarm me, and even though I keep buying ammo, it doesn't feel like bullets are the real problem. It feels like I'm missing some kind of strategy here. The game is intense and fun but stage two is already making me question my life choices

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

First of all stage 2 questioning your life choices is a very real experience.

You’re right that bullets usually aren’t the real problem there. Positioning, pacing, and how you enter spaces matter a lot more than just buying more ammo. The convenience store can punish players who rush in too fast or stay in tight spots for too long.

Try slowing down a bit before entering, checking angles, and making sure you have space to retreat. Creating breathing room is often more important than firing nonstop.

I’m really glad you’re still having fun despite the intensity.
Once the strategy clicks, stage 2 feels completely different.

Stick with it
you’ve got this.

reviews.review_description: I love how this doesn’t feel like a copy-paste game. I think the locations give it a fresh vibe and the pacing is good. Controls took a few minutes to get used to but after that it was smooth. Would love coop someday. Oh and the tutorials is kind of hard...

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Thank you so much for the 5/5 that really means a lot.

I’m glad the locations and pacing stood out to you. I wanted the world to feel distinct rather than copy-paste, so hearing that is incredibly motivating.

Controls can take a few minutes to get used to, especially in VR, but I’m happy it felt smooth once it clicked. I’m also working on improving the tutorial so the early experience feels clearer without losing the tension.

And good news 2-player co-op is already available. You don’t have to survive alone anymore.

Thank you for the thoughtful feedback and support.

reviews.review_description: Game is dark and scary. Set brightness mid-high before play. I accidentally set it too low and I couldn't see much during my first round. It was thrilling at first and became more challenging every stage. Resources are rare... bats are the best

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

That’s a very good tip
brightness can definitely change the experience.

The game is meant to feel dark and tense, but if the brightness is set too low it can become frustrating instead of atmospheric. Adjusting it to a mid-high level is a smart move, especially for a first run.

I’m glad you felt the challenge increasing with each stage. Resource scarcity is intentional to push players toward smart decisions rather than constant shooting.

Thanks for sharing your experience and the brightness advice.

reviews.review_description: Got into my jumpy mode... almost broke the couch. Not used to horror games and honestly it didn't even seem scary... don't know why I was being a scared little doodoo. Almost broke my TV too, thanx bat!

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Almost breaking the couch and the TV on day one is a full VR initiation.

It might not look that scary at first, but once you’re inside the headset, your brain reacts very differently. That jumpy mode is real.

Just make sure you’ve got a bit of space around you while playing
I don’t want ZOMVIRUS responsible for broken furniture.

Thanks for sharing that, and welcome to horror VR.

reviews.review_description: OMG THIS GAME IS INSANE!!! The tension is real when zombies come at you! 😱 Hitting them with melee is literally the best part ever. And the guns?? Loading them feels SO REAL. You guys have to play this! Highly recommend!!

developer_response.body: "Thanks for the hype! 🔥 We are thrilled to hear you're enjoying the intense action and realistic shooting mechanics. Making the tension feel real when zombies swarm is exactly what we aimed for in this survival experience. Reloading is key to staying alive in the apocalypse! Thanks for recommending our shooter to others!"

reviews.review_description: But dang did the streets of Korea look good! Shooting that horrendous pistol was so much fun. I was messing up my aim... guessing Korean men will do better (nearly all of them having served in he military)

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I’m really glad the streets of Korea stood out to you a lot of care went into capturing that atmosphere.

that pistol can feel pretty wild under pressure. Messing up your aim in VR is part of the fun especially when things get chaotic.

As for doing better, survival in ZOMVIRUS is less about background and more about staying calm when everything goes wrong.

Thanks for giving it a chance and sharing your experience.

reviews.review_description: The atmosphere and stage design are really strong, but things can get overwhelming pretty fast once zombies start flooding in. Sometimes it feels like you’re missing information rather than making a mistake. A bit more guidance would make the experience feel fairer, especially early on. Besides that everything seemed good.

developer_response.body:
Hongdae!!!

Thank you for the thoughtful feedback.
I’m really glad you felt the atmosphere and stage design were strong.

You’re absolutely right that things can become overwhelming quickly once the zombies start flooding in. The intensity is intentional, but it should feel like a challenge not like you’re missing information.

I’m currently looking into ways to improve early guidance and feedback, especially for new players, so the experience feels fair while still keeping the tension.

Thank you for pointing that out and for giving the game a chance.

reviews.review_description: This is my first VR shooting game and I think I might have picked something a bit (just a bit) too scary to start with. It's fun, but also very very intense, and it feels like you really need to think strategically. Can't just rush in and shoot.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

That’s actually an amazing first VR shooter to start with, even if it’s a little terrifying.
ZOMVIRUS is definitely designed to be intense, and I’m really glad you noticed that you can’t just rush in and shoot.
Thinking, positioning, and reacting under pressure are a big part of the experience.

Thank you so much for the recommendation and for jumping into something this intense as your first VR shooter.
You handled it better than you think.

reviews.review_description: Having a blast widdit! I think I was sweatin at one point. The only thing was that arrow that kept pointing downward in the beginning. I did finally figure it out.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I’m really glad you’re enjoying it sweating is part of the full experience.

About the arrow pointing downward at the beginning — that was part of the earlier tutorial setup. The onboarding flow has since been reworked, and it’s now handled through the in-room TV tutorial instead.

Thanks for sticking with it and figuring it out anyway. I’m continuing to refine the early experience so it feels clearer without slowing things down.

Appreciate you playing.

reviews.review_description: okay but why is this game so stressful 😭 there's so much to look at and I keep stopping just to check rooms and corners because I am super scared and I may miss something. It feels like exploring matters, not just shooting. Wish there are more ways to explore safely

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

“Omg stressful” is honestly a badge of honor for this game.

That constant tension checking rooms, peeking corners, feeling like you might miss something is exactly the kind of atmosphere I wanted to create. Exploring should matter just as much as shooting.

That said, I understand what you mean about wanting safer ways to explore. I’m looking into ways to improve early tools and feedback so players can manage risk without losing the fear factor.

Thank you for diving into the chaos and surviving it.

reviews.review_description: It's clear the game is still in early access, and there are moments where performance and overall depth could use some improvements. However, the game still manages to maintain tension. The level layout and interactive environment do a good job of keeping the experience engaging.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Thank you for the honest and balanced feedback.

You’re absolutely right
as an early access title, there are still areas where performance and depth can improve. That’s something I’m actively working on.

I’m really glad the tension and level design stood out to you. Keeping the experience engaging through layout and environment interaction is a big part of the game’s identity.

I appreciate you recognizing both the strengths and the areas that need work. That kind of feedback helps shape the future of the game.

reviews.review_description: PLEASE ADD MULTIPLAYER 😭🙏 I’M ACTUALLY BEGGING. I LOVE THIS GAME SO MUCH. I DON’T CARE THAT I KEEP DYING OR GETTING SWARMED — I’M NEW AND I’M STILL HAVING SO MUCH FUN 😭THIS WORLD IS TOO SCARY AND TOO GOOD TO EXPERIENCE ALONE 😭 PLEASE LET ME SURVIVE WITH FRIENDS.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I love this energy.

Good news 2-player co-op mode is already available.

You don’t have to survive this world alone anymore. You can jump in with a friend and experience the chaos together. And honestly, getting swarmed is way more fun (and slightly less terrifying) when someone is screaming next to you.

Thank you for the passion. I’m really glad you’re having fun even while dying that’s the spirit.

Now go survive with friends.

reviews.review_description: I was playing just killing them zombies and later in the game I realized killing zombies gave me something. I found out that I could open fruit cans! I was like WATDAF*&?!

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I love that reaction.

That moment when you realize zombies actually give you resources and that you can open fruit cans is one of those “wait… what?” discoveries I hoped players would have.

The game doesn’t always explain everything upfront, so some systems are meant to click naturally as you keep playing.

Glad you kept going and found it. Those little realizations make survival way more satisfying.

Thanks for sticking with it.

reviews.review_description: So far very good!  zombies felt a little slow but was still fun shooting at them like crazy. Still don't get the numbers on the gun though.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I’m really glad your first day with the game has been a good one.

The zombies can feel a bit slow at first, but as you progress their pressure and behavior shifts. I’m continuing to tune pacing so it feels intense without being overwhelming.

About the numbers on the gun those represent your current ammo and maximum ammo capacity. I understand it may not be immediately clear, and I’m planning to improve the UI feedback so it’s more intuitive.

Thanks for jumping in and sharing your thoughts. There’s more depth as you go.

reviews.review_description: the lobby almost made me quit but the actual stages are really well made. Thought Korea was one of the safest places to walk around at night!

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Thank you for sticking with it.

I’m sorry the lobby almost made you quit that’s definitely not the first impression I want players to have. I’m actively working on improving the tutorial and onboarding so the start feels clearer and smoother.

I’m really glad you pushed through and found the actual stages well made.

Korea is usually one of the safest places to walk around at night. Just not in ZOMVIRUS.

Thanks for giving it a chance.

reviews.review_description: If you like games that keep you on your toes, you should try this one. It’s chaotic. Every stage feels different once things start going wrong. Not the kind of game you play to relax, but definitely one you’ll talk about after.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I really love how you described it.

“Not the kind of game you play to relax, but one you’ll talk about after” that’s honestly the perfect summary of what I hoped ZOMVIRUS would feel like.

The chaos, the unpredictability, and that moment when everything starts going wrong… that’s where the identity of the game really shows.

Thank you for playing and for putting the experience into words so well.
It means a lot.

reviews.review_description: Stages are really well structured. You need to find out how to avoid zombies and where to hide before running into them. Second stage omg there are coming in waves.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I really like the way you described it “brain work” is exactly what I was aiming for.

The stages are built to reward awareness, positioning, and knowing when to avoid instead of rushing in. Finding safe spots and planning your movement is just as important as shooting.

stage two can feel overwhelming with those waves. It’s meant to pressure you into thinking differently, not just firing nonstop.

Thanks for pushing through and for noticing the structure behind the chaos.

reviews.review_description: This game makes you learn by failing. I keep messing up, but every time I respawn I understand the game a bit more. It's kinda cool how it doesn't hold your hand too much and just lets you figure stuff out.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I really like how you described that.

Learning by failing and slowly understanding the systems each time you respawn is a big part of the experience. I wanted the game to feel tense and earned, not overly guided.

At the same time, I’m working on improving the tutorial and early feedback so new players can understand the basics without losing that “figure it out yourself” feeling.

Thank you for embracing the chaos and sticking with it.

reviews.review_description: I wasn't sure what to write because others already explained the game really well. One thing I haven't figured out yet is how to eat food in the game. I feel lilke this will become more important as I play more.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Thank you for playing and for taking the time to write something.

To eat food in the game, take the item out of your inventory (from your left shoulder bag) and bring it up toward your mouth area. It should trigger the use action automatically.

You’re right
managing food becomes more important as you progress, especially when things get intense. Health and resource timing can make a big difference later on.

If anything about the interaction feels unclear, I’m also working on improving the tutorial and feedback to make it easier to understand.

Thanks for sticking with it
the systems start to click the more you play.

reviews.review_title: I'm sure the devs are making it better
reviews.review_description: A bit more guidance or clearer feedback would help, particularly for new players, without taking away from the tension. Still, the pressure and unpredictability are what give the game its identity.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Thank you for such a thoughtful comment.

You’re absolutely right — clearer guidance and feedback, especially for new players, can improve the experience without removing the tension. That balance is something I’m actively working on.

At the same time, I’m really glad you see that the pressure and unpredictability are part of the game’s identity. That feeling of being slightly overwhelmed is intentional, but it should never feel unfair.

I truly appreciate you pointing that out. Feedback like this helps shape the direction of future updates.

reviews.review_description: I thought this would be a pretty straightforward zombie game! BUT it is more like a survival game. You can't just spray bullets and move on. You have to think about where you stand and what you do next!!!

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I love this description.

It may look like a straightforward zombie shooter at first, but it’s definitely closer to survival once things start unfolding. Positioning, timing, and decision-making matter just as much as pulling the trigger.

I’m really glad you picked up on that.
Thanks for taking the time to share your experience.

reviews.review_description: Problem alert pops up frequently when returned to lobby. Please fix this.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Thank you for reporting this.

There was an issue where an error alert could pop up when returning to the lobby. This has already been fixed in the latest update.

Please make sure your game is updated to the newest version, and let me know if the problem still occurs.

Thanks for helping improve the game.

reviews.review_title: No regrests trying this Early Access
reviews.review_description: I hesistated a bit since this is still an early access, but it turned out to be a decision I don't regreat at all. So please, developers if you are reading this, make more stages

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Reading this made my day.

I know Early Access can make people hesitate, so hearing that you don’t regret giving it a try means a lot. Thank you for taking that chance.

And yes, more stages are definitely part of the plan. I’m continuing to expand the game step by step, building on what’s already there while adding new experiences.

I truly appreciate your support and your patience as the game grows.

reviews.review_description: I don't know if others are getting this, but the zombies kinda stay there standing not doing anything. I know that they are suppose to run at you because thats what they did at first

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Thank you for reporting this.

There was an issue in earlier versions where zombies could occasionally stop reacting properly instead of chasing the player. The AI behavior system has since been reworked and improved, and they should now move and react much more consistently.

If you’re still experiencing zombies standing still in the current version, please let me know which headset and version you’re using so I can investigate further.

Thanks for helping improve the game.

reviews.review_description: Maybe it's cause I didn't have much expectations? and I just wanted to play a random game but boy is it fun. I love it when I find gems like this out of nowhere.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I love hearing that.

Sometimes the best experiences come from low expectations and a random click.
Finding out it’s actually fun after that makes it even better.

I’m really happy ZOMVIRUS turned out to be one of those unexpected gems for you.
Thanks for giving it a chance and taking the time to share this.

reviews.review_description: The game reminded me of when I was there. Of course the map wasn't perfect from my memory but had very similar vibes. Can't stop using that shot gun and bat!

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

YES! HONGDAE!

Hearing that the map reminded you of your time there honestly makes me really happy.
It may not be a perfect replica, but capturing that chaotic, late-night Hongdae vibe was exactly the goal.

Thank you for playing and for bringing a bit of your own memories into the game.

reviews.review_description: For an early access game, this one already ha a good variety of stages, which makes it nice to jump into different situations. but also with that, the game does feel a bit heavy

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Thank you for the thoughtful feedback.

I’m really glad you feel there’s already a good variety of stages in early access
creating different situations and pacing shifts was important to me.

You’re also right about the game feeling a bit heavy at times. Performance and overall optimization are ongoing priorities, and I’m continuing to refine things to make the experience smoother without losing the tension.

I appreciate you taking the time to point that out.

reviews.review_description: It is rewarding VR shooter that forces you to think strategically and protect yourself, not just shoot everything in sight.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Thank you so much.
I’m really glad you noticed that the game is meant to be more than just shooting everything in sight.

ZOMVIRUS was designed to reward positioning, awareness, and smart decisions under pressure.
Hearing that you felt that strategic layer means a lot to me.

Thanks for playing and taking the time to share your thoughts.

reviews.review_description: I am super excited about this game. I think about how to beat each stage even off device. Has anybody survived through the third stage? I die everytime I dive in!

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

people have survived stage three… but it doesn’t forgive rushing in. Diving straight into the chaos is usually what gets players overwhelmed.

Stage three rewards patience, positioning, and knowing when to back off just as much as pulling the trigger.

Stick with it. The moment it clicks and you finally push through feels incredibly satisfying.

You’ve got this.

reviews.review_description: The game is good, the only thing that annoyed me was when I accidentally drop the mag, it's so hard to pick it back up.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Thank you for pointing that out.

In the current version, dropped magazines should be pickable again, so you’re not meant to permanently lose them. However, I understand that in intense moments it can still feel frustrating if they fall in an awkward spot.

Could you let me know what exactly happens in your case?
For example, is the magazine not registering when you try to grab it, or does it fall somewhere difficult to reach?

I’d like to understand the exact situation so I can improve it further if needed.

Thank you for the feedback.

reviews.review_description: Details are amazing. Each stage is unique and challenging. Stuck at the dancing club for now but will move on soon!

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Thank you so much.

I’m really happy you noticed the embedded details a lot of care went into making each stage feel distinct and alive.

And yes… the dancing club has humbled quite a few players.
It’s meant to shift the pace and pressure a bit, but I’m glad you’re pushing through it.

You’ll get past it and when you do, it feels earned.

Thanks for sticking with it.

reviews.review_description: Dudes were on steroids before being zombified. Need more like them, kind of like bosses.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

“On steroids before being zombified” made me laugh.

I’m glad those bigger zombies left an impression. They’re meant to shift the pace and force you to react differently compared to regular ones.

More variations especially boss-like encounters are definitely something I’m interested in exploring further. Moments that suddenly change the rhythm of a stage can make things a lot more intense.

Thanks for the idea and for surviving them.

reviews.review_description: Halfway through th 11 stages already. Excited to see what's next!

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Nice

I’m glad you’re pushing through the 11 stages. Things tend to shift as you go deeper, so I hope what’s coming next keeps you on your toes.

Thanks for sticking with it and enjoying the ride. There’s more evolving ahead.

reviews.review_description: I was about to get angry cause I blasted though the game. But there's an update! Hooray!

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

That timing could not have been better.
Right when you were about to get angry… boom, update.

I’m really glad you’re enjoying the game enough to blast through it that fast.
More content and improvements are on the way, so hopefully the next time you finish it, there’ll be even more waiting for you.

Thanks for sticking with it.

reviews.review_description: Some stages are a breeze, some u just gotta push through (club).

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

The difficulty is meant to shift from stage to stage, but I know the club area in particular can feel like you really have to push through it. That tension spike is intentional, though I’m still tuning balance and flow to make sure it feels challenging
not frustrating.

Thanks for sticking with it and making it through.

reviews.review_description: I had so much fun with the shot gun. Get it when you can!

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I’m really glad you had fun with the gunplay.

There’s definitely something satisfying about getting your hands on stronger firepower and just letting it rip when things get intense.

Thanks for sharing your experience and encouraging others to jump in. I’m happy you’re enjoying it.

reviews.review_description: How can I use the inventory bag? Do I take it with me?

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

You don’t need to carry the inventory bag manually.
You can access it by reaching over your left shoulder and pulling it out during gameplay.

If that wasn’t clear at the start, that’s on me
I’m currently working on improving the tutorial so new players can understand these interactions more easily.

Thanks for asking, and I hope that helps.

reviews.review_title: Good experience I had with this one
reviews.review_description: All good. Fun. Love when racking the gun!

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I’m really glad you had a good experience with it.

there’s something incredibly satisfying about racking the gun. That tactile feeling is a big part of what makes VR special, so I’m happy you enjoyed it.

Thanks for playing and sharing your thoughts.

reviews.review_description: Seriously, there is no end to them

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

It definitely feels that way sometimes.

The waves are designed to create pressure and make you feel overwhelmed, especially if you stay in one spot for too long. There is an end to them but surviving long enough and managing space is part of the challenge.

Sometimes repositioning or creating distance makes a huge difference compared to just standing and shooting.

Thanks for diving into the chaos.

reviews.review_description: This game stressed me out more than I expected. Fumbling a reload in VR is brutal. I liked how the setting was more on the realistic side instead of cartoony, which made everything feel more intense. Had a few moments where I had to take the headset off and breathe lol. If you like horror and shooters, this hits both.(⓿_⓿)

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

“Had to take the headset off and breathe” that’s when you know VR is doing its job.

Fumbling a reload under pressure is brutal, but that’s part of what makes it feel real. I’m really glad the more grounded, realistic setting worked for you. The goal was to make every mistake feel intense, not just cosmetic.

If it made you stressed, tired… and still think it’s awesome, then I’ll take that as a win.

Thanks for diving into both the horror and the shooter side of it.

reviews.review_description: the scene is really scary. it says the multiplayer mode will be eventually updated. cant go alone and waiting to play with my friends.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

I don’t blame you some scenes are definitely not meant to be faced alone.

Good news: 2-player co-op mode is already available. The multiplayer update has been rolled out, so you can jump in with a friend and survive together instead of waiting.

Everything feels different when you’ve got someone watching your back.

Hope you and your squad dive in soon.

reviews.review_description: Has anybody beaten stage3? Is there a community where I can get some hints? I hid between busses for stage 2.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Stage 3 has definitely stopped quite a few players you’re not alone.

Quick reload timing becomes very important there. Managing space and keeping your reloads smooth under pressure can make a huge difference.

If manual reloading feels overwhelming, you can enable the auto-reload option in the settings. Some players use it while learning the stage, then switch back once they feel more confident.

I’m also continuing to tune the difficulty balance, especially around stages 2 and 3, to keep them challenging but fair.

And yes, hiding between the buses in stage 2 is actually a smart move.

Stick with it once stage 3 clicks, it feels very satisfying.

reviews.review_description: Each stage makes a player to try and fail and make strategy.

developer_response.body:
Hello, this is Jooyoo, the developer of ZOMVIRUS.

Thank you for reporting this issue.
I believe I’ve identified the cause of the shoulder holster problem where the hand disappears when grabbing the rifle.

This is not intended behavior, and I understand how it makes the game unplayable.
I’m working on a fix right away and will include it in the next update as soon as possible.

Thank you for your patience and for bringing this to my attention.
